info.name: Restful Booker API
info.description:
Full test suite for https://restful-booker.herokuapp.com — covers all 7 endpoints with happy path, auth protection, edge cases, and known API quirks.

Setup requests (prefixed [Setup]) create test data; the following request is the actual assertion.
Known API bugs are noted in test names and assertions.

exec: // Auto-fetch a token before every request if one is not already stored.
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ username: 'admin', password: 'password123' }) }

item.name: Health
RB-H-001 Ping — responds with 201

exec: pm.test('Status is 201 (known issue: should be 200)', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(201); });

item.name: Auth
RB-A-001 CreateToken — valid credentials return token
RB-A-002 CreateToken — wrong password returns reason (known issue: should be 401)

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (known issue: should be 401)', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Body has reason: Bad credentials', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.json().reason).to.equal('Bad credentials'); });

item.name: RB-A-003 CreateToken — wrong username returns reason

exec: pm.test('Body has reason: Bad credentials', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.json().reason).to.equal('Bad credentials'); });

item.name: RB-A-004 CreateToken — missing password returns reason
RB-A-005 CreateToken — empty body returns reason
RB-A-006 CreateToken — SQL injection returns reason

exec: pm.test('SQL injection does not bypass auth', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.json().reason).to.equal('Bad credentials'); });

GetBooking

exec: pm.test('Status is 500 (known issue: should be 400)', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(500); });

item.name: [Setup] RB-P-005 — Create booking for wrong-type test
RB-P-005 PartialUpdate — wrong type silently nullifies field (known issue: should be 400)

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (known issue: should be 400)', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('totalprice is null due to type mismatch', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.json().totalprice).to.be.null; });

pm.sendRequest({ url: pm.collectionVariables.get('baseUrl') + '/booking/' + pm.environment.get('testBookingId'), method: 'DELETE', header: [{ key: 'Cookie', value: 'token=' + pm.environment.get('token') }] }, () => {});

item.name: [Setup] RB-P-006 — Create booking for invalid-date test
RB-P-006 PartialUpdate — checkout before checkin accepted (known issue: should be 400)

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (known issue: should be 400)', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

pm.sendRequest({ url: pm.collectionVariables.get('baseUrl') + '/booking/' + pm.environment.get('testBookingId'), method: 'DELETE', header: [{ key: 'Cookie', value: 'token=' + pm.environment.get('token') }] }, () => {});

item.name: CreateBooking — Data Driven
item.description:
Runs POST /booking once per row in create-booking-testdata.json.
Pass -d postman/data/create-booking-testdata.json to Newman, or run: npm run test:api:data
When no data file is supplied the requests are automatically skipped.

item.name: CreateBooking — {{description}}

exec: // skip this request when running without a data file
pm.test(`[${desc}] Booking object contains submitted fields`, () => {

item.name: Chained Flow
item.description:
End-to-end booking lifecycle showing explicit data flow:
  Step 1: POST /auth          → produces token
  Step 2: POST /booking       → produces bookingId
  Step 3: GET  /booking/:id   → consumes bookingId
  Step 4: PUT  /booking/:id   → consumes bookingId + token
  Step 5: GET  /booking/:id   → verify PUT
  Step 6: PATCH /booking/:id  → consumes bookingId + token
  Step 7: GET  /booking/:id   → verify PATCH
  Step 8: DELETE /booking/:id → consumes bookingId + token
  Step 9: GET  /booking/:id   → verify deleted (404)

item.name: Step 1 - Authenticate and retrieve token
Step 2 - Create a new booking
Step 3 - Retrieve the created booking
